title: oxytropis sericea
intro: Oxytropis sericea, also known as the silky globeflower or silky locoweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family. Traditionally, the roots of Oxytropis sericea have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Oxytropis sericea include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and soothe respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Oxytropis sericea often involve boiling the roots to create a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
